question_text: An astronaut has a mass of 80 kg. On Earth, gravitational field strength is 10 N/kg, but on the Moon it is only 1.6 N/kg. Calculate the astronaut's weight on Earth and on the Moon, then explain why the astronaut's mass stays the same but weight changes.
correct_answer: Weight on Earth = 800 N; Weight on Moon = 128 N. Mass stays the same (80 kg) because it is the amount of material in an object, but weight changes because gravitational field strength is different on the Moon.
explanation: Weight is calculated using W = m × g. On Earth: 80 × 10 = 800 N. On the Moon: 80 × 1.6 = 128 N. Mass is constant but weight depends on the local gravitational field strength, which is weaker on the Moon.
